item.title: 15 of the Best Anemone Varieties for Spring, Summer, and Fall
item.description:
Anemones are easy to care for and produce long-lasting, bright flowers, some are daisy-like while others resemble poppies. Depending on the variety, they flower in spring, summer, and fall, can be planted in sun or light shade, and make wonderful cut flowers. Learn about 15 of the best anemone varieties now.

item.title: 21 of the Best Plants to Grow on a Septic Discharge Field
item.description:
A septic field is a necessary evil but it doesn’t need to be an eyesore. Most people opt for a lawn to cover their field, but there are many other options. While you don’t want to plant any trees or shrubs over the area, you can plant something pretty. This guide gives you 21 options for dressing up your drainage area.

The post 21 of the Best Plants to Grow on a Septic Discharge Field appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: How to Plant and Grow Daikon Radish
item.description:
If you’re looking to add some zing to your fall garden, look no further than the daikon radish. With white roots that can weigh over a pound, these large radishes are low maintenance and easy to grow. While they are widely grown and used in parts of Asia, they can be grown elsewhere. Read more now.

The post How to Plant and Grow Daikon Radish appeared first on Gardener's Path.
